---
title: "KD-GAT: CAN Bus Intrusion Detection via Knowledge Distillation"
subtitle: "Graph-Based Anomaly Detection with Model Compression for Edge Deployment"
author:
  - name: Robert Frenken
    affiliation: The Ohio State University
date: 2026-02-25
format:
  html: default
---

## About

KD-GAT is a three-stage pipeline for CAN bus intrusion detection that combines graph neural networks with knowledge distillation for deployment on resource-constrained automotive edge devices.

The pipeline uses:

1. **VGAE** (Variational Graph Autoencoder) --- unsupervised graph reconstruction to identify hard examples
2. **GAT** (Graph Attention Network) --- supervised classification with curriculum learning
3. **DQN** (Deep Q-Network) --- reinforcement learning fusion of model predictions

Large teacher models are compressed into small student models via knowledge distillation, achieving competitive performance at ~20$\times$ parameter reduction. We evaluate on six CAN bus datasets spanning DoS, fuzzing, spoofing, suppress, and masquerade attack types.

## Navigate

| Section | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [Dashboard](dashboard.qmd) | Interactive experiment dashboard with Mosaic/vgplot visualizations |
| [Paper](paper/paper.qmd) | Full research paper (10 chapters) |
| [Slides](slides.qmd) | Revealjs presentation |

## Paper Chapters

1. [Introduction](paper/paper.qmd#introduction)
2. [Background](paper/paper.qmd#background)
3. [Related Work](paper/paper.qmd#related-work)
4. [Methodology](paper/paper.qmd#methodology-overview)
5. [Experiments](paper/paper.qmd#experiments)
6. [Results](paper/paper.qmd#results-and-discussion)
7. [Ablation](paper/paper.qmd#ablation-study)
8. [Explainability](paper/paper.qmd#explainability)
9. [Conclusion](paper/paper.qmd#conclusion)
10. [Appendix](paper/paper.qmd#experimental-setup)
